_meta.notes: Updated to Set 16 (Lore & Legends). Items removed since Set 14: Redemption, Locket, Zeke's Herald, Shroud of Stillness. Spirit Visage added. Categories changed to carry/mage/tank. Icons from Community Dragon CDN.

items.name: Infinity Edge

passive.name: Perfect Timing
passive.description: Abilities can critically strike. If the holder can already critically strike with abilities, gain +10% Critical Strike Damage instead.

items.why_it_matters: Infinity Edge is the cornerstone crit item for AD carries. It unlocks spell crits and piles on damage, making it the default first item for snipers like Caitlyn or Jinx. Stack crit from other sources and every hit becomes massive.

items.name: Giant Slayer

passive.name: Executioner
passive.description: Abilities and attacks deal 15% bonus damage. This bonus increases to 45% against enemies with more than 1600 max HP.

items.why_it_matters: Giant Slayer punishes lobbies that stack HP-heavy tanks. The bonus damage scales directly with how beefy the target is — so the tankier the opponent's frontline, the harder Giant Slayer hits. Build it when you see Bruisers and Juggernauts.

items.name: Bloodthirster

passive.name: Lifeline
passive.description: Once per combat at 40% max Health, gain a shield equal to 25% of max Health that decays over 3 seconds.

items.why_it_matters: Bloodthirster is defensive insurance for your carry. The shield at 40% HP absorbs a burst of damage at the exact moment your carry is most vulnerable — often keeping them alive through an assassin's combo long enough to finish the fight.

items.name: Last Whisper

passive.name: Armor Shred
passive.description: Physical damage Sunders (reduces armor by 30%) the target for 3 seconds.

items.why_it_matters: Last Whisper is a team amplifier disguised as a carry item. Every Sunder on the target means your entire team's physical damage is boosted against them. Essential when the enemy frontline is stacking Armor.
best_on: Comps with multiple physical damage sources

items.name: Guinsoo's Rageblade

passive.name: Guinsoo's Rage
passive.description: Gain +6% Attack Speed per second in combat. Stacks have no cap.

items.why_it_matters: Rageblade turns any fast-attacking champion into a late-fight monster. The longer the combat goes, the faster they attack — snowballing into fights that drag on. Ideal on Quickstriker carries who already scale with attack speed.

items.name: Rabadon's Deathcap

passive.name: Magical Mastery
passive.description: Grants +50 bonus Ability Power. Core item that amplifies every other AP source on the holder.

items.why_it_matters: Rabadon's is a flat AP powerhouse. 80 AP from the item plus the bonus from the passive means your Arcanist carry starts nuking targets immediately. Every other AP item you build becomes stronger by proxy — it's always the first item to consider on any mage.
best_on: Any champion that scales with Ability Power

items.name: Jeweled Gauntlet

passive.name: Spell Crit
passive.description: Abilities can critically strike. If the holder can already critically strike with abilities, gain +10% Critical Strike Damage instead.

items.why_it_matters: By default, TFT abilities cannot crit. Jeweled Gauntlet removes that ceiling entirely. Pair it with Rabadon's and your mage is suddenly one-shotting targets mid-fight. Currently the highest win-rate item in Set 16 — never skip it on Arcanist carries.

items.name: Archangel's Staff

passive.name: Mana to Power
passive.description: Every 5 seconds in combat, gain +20 Ability Power. This effect stacks without limit.

items.why_it_matters: Archangel's is the patience item — it rewards mages who stay alive long enough to ramp. In a 20-second fight, that's +80 bonus AP on top of the base stats. Pair with Invoker trait for extra mana regen and your mage reaches full ramp in seconds.

items.name: Blue Buff

passive.name: Empowered
passive.description: Grants +10% bonus Attack Damage and Ability Power from all sources. This amplification stacks with other sources of bonus AD and AP.

items.why_it_matters: Blue Buff was reworked in Set 14 into a flat amplifier: all bonus AD and AP you receive is increased by 10%. It synergizes with every other item on the holder, making the items you already built stronger. Effectively a mini Rabadon's that works on both AD and AP.
best_on: AP carries with multiple AP items

items.name: Morellonomicon

passive.name: Grievous Wounds
passive.description: Attacks and abilities apply 1% Burn and 33% Wound for 10 seconds, reducing healing received by the target.

items.why_it_matters: Morellonomicon is a hard counter to healing-heavy comps. The 33% Wound effect cuts enemy heals dramatically — critical against Slayer carries who rely on Omnivamp or Bruiser frontlines with healing augments. Always build at least one on your team when you see heavy sustain.
best_on: AoE mages who hit multiple targets

items.name: Warmog's Armor
items.why_it_matters: Warmog's is the simplest and most reliable tank item: a massive HP spike. The bonus max Health passive means every HP item you build afterward becomes slightly more effective too. Stack two on a Bruiser or Juggernaut and they become nearly unkillable.

items.name: Dragon's Claw

passive.name: Wyrmguard
passive.description: Grants +9% bonus max Health. Heals 2.5% of max Health every 2 seconds in combat.

items.why_it_matters: Dragon's Claw makes your frontline nearly immune to AP damage while also giving them constant healing. In Arcanist-heavy lobbies, one Dragon's Claw on your main tank can completely nullify the enemy carry's output while keeping that tank alive indefinitely.

items.name: Gargoyle Stoneplate

passive.name: Stone Form
passive.description: Gain +10 Armor and +10 Magic Resist for each enemy targeting the holder.

items.why_it_matters: Gargoyle Stoneplate is the best item for any tank that draws a lot of fire. The more enemies targeting your tank, the tankier they become — which also means they keep drawing fire even longer. Synergizes perfectly with Warden shields and Juggernaut durability.
best_on: Units with taunt or aggro-drawing abilities

items.name: Sunfire Cape

passive.name: Immolate
passive.description: Grants +8% bonus max Health. Every 2 seconds in combat, applies 1% Burn and 33% Wound to a nearby enemy.

items.why_it_matters: Sunfire Cape gives your frontline passive AoE damage and healing suppression without needing to be a damage dealer. Just having your tank stand near clustered enemies slowly melts them while countering their sustain — great against Bruiser or Slayer frontlines.

items.name: Bramble Vest

passive.name: Thorns
passive.description: Grants +7% bonus max Health. Upon taking physical damage, deal 100 magic damage to nearby enemies. Applies 33% Wound for 3 seconds.

items.why_it_matters: Bramble Vest punishes auto-attack-heavy comps hard. Every hit on your tank deals splash damage to nearby enemies and Wounds them — meaning Gunslinger or Quickstriker carries are literally hurting themselves by attacking your frontline.
best_on: Tanks that position near clustered enemies

items.name: Ionic Spark

passive.name: Zap
passive.description: Shreds the Magic Resist of enemies within 2 hexes by 30%. When a nearby enemy casts their ability, zap them for magic damage scaling with their mana spent.

items.why_it_matters: Ionic Spark is the frontline's answer to Arcanist-heavy lobbies. It shreds enemy MR on every nearby unit constantly — making your entire AP team hit harder — and punishes enemies for casting abilities. A single Ionic Spark tank can amplify your whole mage comp.
best_on: Frontline tanks in AP mage comps
Defender or Warden champions with high MR

items.name: Spirit Visage

passive.name: Blessed
passive.description: Heals the holder for 2.5% of missing Health every second in combat.

items.why_it_matters: Spirit Visage replaced Redemption as the go-to sustain item for tanks. The missing-HP scaling means it heals more when your tank is in danger — effectively acting as a regenerating safety net during long fights. Stack with Warmog's for maximum staying power.
best_on: HP-heavy tanks that take sustained damage
